content.problemStatement:
Suzumo is the coach of the ChefLand OI team. Physical condition is very important in any olympiad, so he wants to make the children run a bit as a warmup.

The team consists of NNN children numbered 111 through NNN standing at some positions on the xxx-axis. For each valid iii, the initial xxx-coordinate of the iii-th kid is xix_ixi​, the running velocity of the iii-th kid is constant and equal to viv_ivi​.

Suzumo wants to assign a running direction (left or right, i.e. in the direction of decreasing or increasing xxx-coordinate) to each kid; the children start running at time 000 in the assigned directions. Afterwards, Suzumo will measure the smallest time ttt at which some kid passes another one. Help Suzumo compute the maximum time ttt if he can assign the directions arbitrarily!

**Note:** Child iii passes child jjj at time tijt_{ij}tij​ if their coordinates satisfy xi<xjx_i < x_jxi​<xj​ at any time t<tijt < t_{ij}t<tij​ and xi>xjx_i > x_jxi​>xj​ at any time t>tijt > t_{ij}t>tij​, or if they satisfy xi>xjx_i > x_jxi​>xj​ at any time t<tijt < t_{ij}t<tij​ and xi<xjx_i < x_jxi​<xj​ at any time t>tijt > t_{ij}t>tij​.
constraints: 1 ≤ xi, vi ≤ 10^9 for each valid i
no two children have the same initial positions
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. N lines follow. For each valid i, the i-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xi and vi.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number — the maximum possible time t, or −1 if it is possible to assign directions in such a way that no kid ever passes another.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One optimal assignment of directions is left, right, right.
The only possible assignment of directions is left, right.
There is only one kid, so there is no one to pass.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first sort the children based on their initial positions. Then, we iterate over all pairs of children and calculate the time it would take for them to pass each other if they were running in opposite directions. We keep track of the minimum time found so far. If we find a pair of children that can never pass each other, we return -1. Otherwise, we return the minimum time found.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) — we iterate over all pairs of children for each test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the positions and velocities of all children
